title: sphaeralcea polychroma
intro: Sphaeralcea polychroma, also known as the Mexican gold poppy or globe mallow, is a flowering perennial plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and infections. The health benefits of Sphaeralcea polychroma include reducing inflammation and improving wound healing, as well as providing antioxidant and antiseptic effects. The plant can be prepared as a tea, salve, or ointment for topical application, or as a tincture for internal use, allowing for its medicinal properties to be harnessed in various herbal preparations.
